title: rubus moluccanus var. reinwardtii
intro: Rubus moluccanus var. reinwardtii, also known as the Moluccan raspberry or Reineck's raspberry, is a species of raspberry native to Southeast Asia. It is a type of bramble with edible fruits and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant has been reported to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating digestive issues, as well as having antimicrobial and antiviral properties. Herbal preparations of Rubus moluccanus var. reinwardtii include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.
